title: phegopteris aurita
intro: Phegopteris aurita, also known as the auriculated polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Phegopteris. It is native to East Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is known to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. Phegopteris aurita can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments, including bronchitis, asthma, and digestive problems.
